name: Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach
name_original: Burela – vuelta por río Covo y la Playa de Marosa
region_intro: Touring cycling around Burela offers diverse landscapes along the scenic Cantabrian coast, characterized by dramatic geological formations and pristine beaches. The region features established routes that integrate seafront promenades and river paths, such as those along the Covo river. Cyclists can also ascend to the Monte Castelo viewpoint, which provides panoramic vistas of the coastline. Best touring cycling routes around Burela The most popular touring cycling route is Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores coastal areas and river paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Marosa Beach – Covo River Bridge loop from Burela, a difficult 44.6 miles (71.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and diverse coastal scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Ría de Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Cangas de Foz, a 66.8 miles (107.5 km) trail leading through coastal rías and beaches, often completed in about 7 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Burela is defined by the Cantabrian coastline, geological formations, and river routes. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Burela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Burela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Burela?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Burela, offering diverse options for exploring the region's coastal and river landscapes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Burela?
A: While many routes in Burela are classified as moderate to difficult due to elevation gains, the region's &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1554639/cycling-around-burela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Circular de Burela&lt;/a&gt; is noted for having sections accessible for wheelchairs and strollers, suggesting a relatively well-maintained and paved surface suitable for less strenuous touring. However, komoot data indicates that most routes are moderate to difficult.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Burela?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marosa Beach – Covo River Bridge loop from Burela&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant challenge, covering 71.8 km with substantial elevation gains. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Cangas de Foz&lt;/a&gt;, which extends over 107 km through coastal rías and beaches.

Q: Are there long-distance touring cycling options from Burela?
A: Yes, Burela is part of the broader Natural Route of the Cantabrian Path, a coastal strip extending over 150 kilometers. This allows for longer touring possibilities, connecting Burela with other towns and beaches in the region. Within the immediate area, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Foz – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Cangas de Foz&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial 107.5 km ride.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Burela?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Burela are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017423035/burela-loop-via-covo-river-and-marosa-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option exploring coastal areas and river paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marosa Beach – Covo River Bridge loop from Burela&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular route.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on touring cycling routes around Burela?
A: Touring cycling around Burela offers diverse landscapes along the scenic Cantabrian coast, characterized by dramatic geological formations and pristine beaches. You'll find routes integrating seafront promenades, river paths along the Covo river, and ascents to viewpoints like Monte Castelo, providing panoramic vistas of the coastline.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling in Burela?
A: While cycling around Burela, you can encounter several interesting attractions. Routes often incorporate the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3171884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Atalaia Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cibrao Fishing Village&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Fazouro&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2317293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covo River Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable feature along some river-based routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Burela?
A: The touring cycling routes in Burela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse coastal scenery, the integration of river paths, and the challenging yet rewarding elevation gains.

Q: Are there routes that follow river paths in Burela?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that incorporate river paths, such as those along the Covo river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017423035/burela-loop-via-covo-river-and-marosa-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela - loop via Covo river and Marosa Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, allowing you to experience a different dimension of the cycling landscape.

Q: Are there routes that incorporate historical elements in Burela?
A: Yes, some routes in the area combine natural beauty with historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39838282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape and castro of Burela – Marosa Beach loop from Cervo&lt;/a&gt; incorporates the Cape and Castro of Burela, offering a glimpse into the region's past alongside scenic coastal views.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: A prominent viewpoint often included in local cycling circuits is the Monte Castelo viewpoint. It offers panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian coastline, providing stunning photo opportunities and a rewarding stop during your ride.
tips: From the Covo river bridge you can see the San Cibrao view with its row of buildings on the riverbank and the boats along the boardwalk.
A narrow road leads down across the railroad tracks to the sandy beach cove.
Exquisite rocks frame the sandy beach cove
Soft sandy protected beach to the north of the small coastal fishing town of Burela.
Well protected cove with a sandy beach between two spits of rock reaching far out into the water.
Burela is a small, but very active, fishing port. In its surroundings you will find the typical green landscape of the Galician coast facing the Bay of Biscay and its dream beaches.
